_meta.icp: AI Automation Agency - Mid-Market B2B Buyers (50-1000 emp)
_meta.seller_offering: Done-for-you AI automation agency: voice agents, chatbots, RAG, AEO, speed-to-lead.
_meta.buyer_rule: A buyer CONSUMES automation. A vendor/competitor SELLS automation, RevOps, or AI-workflow products. A SaaS selling something else (compliance, planning, content, banking) is a buyer.
_meta.scoring_assertion: expected_grade_band is non-null ONLY for buyer and too_large. Reject classes (vendor/competitor/investor_vc/job_board_or_directory/listicle_or_content) are the gate's responsibility, asserted by eval_qualification, not scoring.
_meta.grade_band_syntax: Range string like 'A-B', 'C-D', 'D-F', or single 'F'. null = scoring not asserted for this row.

companies.name: Synctera
companies.title: Synctera - Banking as a Service Platform for Fintechs
companies.snippet: Synctera helps companies launch and scale fintech products with sponsor banks, compliance, and ledger infrastructure.
companies.notes: Sells BaaS infrastructure, not automation. Proven A in live pipeline.

companies.name: Vanta
companies.title: Vanta - Automated Security and Compliance
companies.snippet: Vanta helps companies achieve and maintain SOC 2, ISO 27001, and HIPAA compliance. Ops-heavy mid-market SaaS.
companies.notes: Sells compliance, NOT automation -> buyer. 'Automated compliance' wording is a trap; core product is compliance.

companies.name: Pigment
companies.title: Pigment - Business Planning Platform
companies.snippet: Pigment is a business planning and forecasting platform for finance, sales, and HR teams scaling operations.
companies.notes: Sells planning, not automation -> buyer.

companies.name: Liquid Death
companies.title: Liquid Death - Canned Water Beverage Brand
companies.snippet: Liquid Death is a fast-growing DTC beverage brand selling canned water and iced tea, scaling retail and ecommerce ops.
companies.notes: Clean operating-company buyer. DTC brand, obviously consumes (does not sell) automation.

companies.name: Tend
companies.title: Tend - Modern Dental Care, Multiple Locations
companies.snippet: Tend operates dental studios across several US cities, handling patient intake, scheduling, and billing across locations.
companies.notes: Multi-site healthcare PROVIDER (operates clinics), not a health-tech vendor. Consumes automation for intake/scheduling.

companies.name: Newfront
companies.title: Newfront - Commercial Insurance Brokerage
companies.snippet: Newfront is a commercial insurance brokerage providing business insurance, benefits, and risk management to companies.
companies.notes: Brokerage SELLS insurance, not automation -> buyer. Heavy manual quoting/back-office = automation-hungry.

companies.name: Mercury
companies.title: Mercury - Banking for Startups
companies.snippet: Mercury provides banking and financial services tailored for startups and tech companies.
companies.notes: Deliberate cap-EDGE case at ~1000. Verify headcount; if >1000 it flips to too_large.

companies.name: Stellar
companies.title: Stellar - Customer Engagement Software
companies.snippet: Stellar offers customer engagement and retention software for consumer brands.
companies.notes: In-band SaaS buyer, weaker signals -> B/C.

companies.name: Jasper
companies.title: Jasper - AI Content Platform for Marketing Teams
companies.snippet: Jasper helps marketing teams create on-brand content with AI. Mid-market marketing SaaS.
companies.notes: AI-adjacent but sells CONTENT generation, not workflow automation -> softer buyer (B/C).

companies.name: OroCommerce
companies.title: OroCommerce - B2B eCommerce Platform
companies.snippet: OroCommerce is a B2B ecommerce platform for manufacturers, distributors, and wholesalers.
companies.notes: Borderline buyer flagged live. In-band, real CRO-hire signal, weak automation-stack match -> B/C.

companies.name: Automattic
companies.title: Automattic - The Company Behind WordPress.com
companies.snippet: Automattic makes WordPress.com, WooCommerce, Tumblr, and Jetpack. $700M+ ARR software company.
companies.notes: Just over the 1000 cap -> MILD size penalty -> C/D. Tests the graduated curve at the low end.

companies.name: UiPath
companies.title: UiPath - Enterprise Automation and RPA Platform
companies.snippet: UiPath is an enterprise robotic process automation and AI platform used by large organizations worldwide.
companies.notes: RPA vendor BUT caught primarily by SIZE (~4000). Tests mid-range size penalty. (Vendor-by-product is also true; size dominates.)

companies.name: Stripe
companies.title: Stripe - Payments Infrastructure for the Internet
companies.snippet: Stripe is a global payments and financial infrastructure platform serving millions of businesses.
companies.notes: Giant fintech (~8000). Graded band is informational; reject-class ASSERTION is the principled guard: score < B AND < the lowest buyer score (size penalty keeps it rejected even with strong signals).

companies.name: Rockwell Automation
companies.title: Rockwell Automation - Industrial Automation and Digital Transformation
companies.snippet: Rockwell Automation is a global industrial automation and information company with ~27,000 employees.
companies.notes: Canonical failure: 27x over cap. Graded band informational; reject-class ASSERTION is score < B AND < lowest buyer. Industry 'Manufacturing' is in-ICP so industry bonus is present -> tests that size penalty overrides it.

companies.name: Notable
companies.title: Notable - Healthcare Workflow Automation Platform
companies.snippet: Notable automates healthcare administrative workflows with AI for health systems: intake, scheduling, billing.
companies.notes: SELLS healthcare workflow automation -> vendor, NOT buyer. In-band -> size can't catch it; GATE must. Was wrongly an A anchor.

companies.name: Clari
companies.title: Clari - Revenue Operations and RevOps Platform
companies.snippet: Clari is a revenue operations platform automating forecasting, pipeline, and revenue workflows for sales teams.
companies.notes: SELLS RevOps/revenue-workflow automation -> vendor. In-band (~600) -> pure GATE test.

companies.name: Zapier
companies.title: Zapier - Automation for Busy People
companies.snippet: Zapier connects your apps and automates workflows without code. Thousands of integrations.
companies.notes: Automation PLATFORM vendor, in-band (~800). Firmographically a perfect 'buyer' -> the key in-band gate trap.

companies.name: Make
companies.title: Make - Visual Automation Platform (formerly Integromat)
companies.snippet: Make lets you design, build, and automate workflows and integrations visually without code.
companies.notes: Automation platform vendor, in-band (~300). GATE must catch.

companies.name: Trust Automation
companies.title: Trust Automation - Motion Control and Automation Systems
companies.snippet: Trust Automation designs and builds motion control and automation systems for defense, medical, and industrial uses.
companies.notes: Industrial automation MAKER, in-band (~150) -> SLIPPED TO A live. Size can't catch; GATE must via what-they-do.

companies.name: VoltixIO
companies.title: VoltixIO - Done-For-You AI Automation Agency
companies.snippet: VoltixIO builds AI voice agents that book appointments, qualify leads, and handle calls 24/7. DFY setup from $3,000.
companies.notes: DIRECT competitor: DFY AI automation agency selling voice agents + speed-to-lead. ~30 emp = identical firmographics to a small buyer -> hardest class; classifier must reason about what they DO.

companies.name: JOROPI
companies.title: JOROPI - AI Voice Agents & Automation for Service Businesses
companies.snippet: JOROPI builds AI systems that respond to leads in under 30 seconds, qualify prospects, and book appointments.
companies.notes: DIRECT competitor: DFY agency, speed-to-lead + appointment booking. Pure what-they-do gate test.

companies.name: LvlUp Ventures
companies.title: LvlUp Ventures - Early-Stage Venture Capital Firm
companies.snippet: LvlUp Ventures invests in early-stage B2B SaaS, e-commerce, and CPG startups across multiple niche funds.
companies.notes: VC firm -> not a buyer. Live false positive. Emp corrected to ~30 (576 was a bad scrape; VCs are tiny).

companies.name: Andreessen Horowitz
companies.title: Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) - Venture Capital
companies.snippet: a16z is a venture capital firm backing bold entrepreneurs building the future through technology.
companies.notes: VC, IN-BAND (~500) -> size cannot catch; GATE must catch investor by what-it-is.

companies.name: Y Combinator
companies.title: Y Combinator - Startup Accelerator
companies.snippet: Y Combinator is a startup accelerator and investor; also hosts a startup directory and job board.
companies.notes: Accelerator/investor + directory. In-band-ish; GATE must catch.

companies.name: Automate America
companies.title: Automate America - Automation Engineering Jobs and Staffing
companies.snippet: Automate America connects automation and robotics engineers with contract jobs. Browse open positions.
companies.notes: Automation JOBS board. Live false positive. Tiny (~9) -> size floor also backstops, but URL /jobs + content should catch deterministically.

companies.name: ZipRecruiter
companies.title: ZipRecruiter - Online Employment Marketplace
companies.snippet: ZipRecruiter is an online job board connecting employers and job seekers.
companies.notes: Job board. Known junk domain -> deterministic catch.

companies.name: G2
companies.title: G2 - Business Software Reviews and Comparisons
companies.snippet: G2 is a software marketplace and review directory where buyers compare business software.
companies.notes: Software DIRECTORY, IN-BAND (~900). Known junk domain; tests directory catch where size can't help.

companies.name: Clutch
companies.title: Clutch - B2B Ratings and Agency Directory
companies.snippet: Clutch is a B2B ratings and reviews directory listing agencies and service providers.
companies.notes: Agency DIRECTORY, in-band (~300). Tests directory catch.

companies.name: Top 10 AI Automation Agencies in 2026
companies.title: Top 10 AI Automation Agencies in 2026 (Ranked)
companies.snippet: We ranked the best AI automation agencies for 2026. Here are the top 10 firms to consider for your business.
companies.notes: Listicle URL, not a company. Title 'Top 10 ... agencies' + content domain -> deterministic catch.

companies.name: What is workflow automation
companies.title: What is Workflow Automation? A Beginner's Guide
companies.snippet: Workflow automation explained: definitions, benefits, and examples for teams getting started.
companies.notes: Explainer/content page on a /blog/ path with 'what is' title -> deterministic catch (even though zapier.com is a real co, THIS URL is content).
